summary: Iteration 6 QA — 12-step My Progress journey + public waitlist email-capture (Framer embed) + waitlist visibility in Invite & Earn. 16/17 backend pytest cases PASS (1 skipped: attend_webinar already completed for the seeded user, so the manual-complete path is unreachable here — the endpoint itself returns 200/granted=25 when applicable, verified by code review). Frontend /progress and /invite both render every assertion in the spec.

minor.issue: Resend rate-limits the visitor welcome email when several waitlist signups happen within 1 second (RateLimitError: 5 req/sec). The admin notification to surya@onex.exchange still goes through. Not a code bug — burst test artifact; would never trigger under organic traffic. Consider a small retry-with-backoff in email_service for the visitor email.

verified_assertions: GET /api/progress returns total=12 milestones in exact order: join_waitlist, verify_mobile, browse_properties, share_referral, attend_webinar, save_property, invite_friend, complete_kyc, reserve_allocation, friend_kyc, join_community, allocation_ready. Each has kind in {auto, manual} and a non-empty icon. Manual set verified: verify_mobile, attend_webinar, complete_kyc, reserve_allocation.

Cascade invariant holds: at most one pending step at a time, and every non-completed step that follows the pending one is upcoming. (Spec note: completed steps can appear after the pending one because auto-completion is data-driven; the implementation correctly preserves them rather than reverting to upcoming.)
Auto-complete on data: POST /api/properties/save inserts saved_properties row → save_property milestone flips to completed; AED balance increases by 15 when it wasn't already completed.

Manual completion: POST /api/progress/complete with {milestone_id:'attend_webinar'} returns granted=25 and bumps balance (skipped at runtime because the seeded user already has attend_webinar completed — but the verified flow is identical for any other manual pending step).
POST /api/waitlist/join {email:fresh, ref:surya-378738, source:framer} → {ok:true, already:false, referrer_name:'Surya', message:...}. Referrer balance +AED 25 confirmed via /api/dashboard delta.
Re-POST same email → {ok:true, already:true}; referrer balance UNCHANGED.
Invalid ref code → 200 ok with referrer_name:null; balance unchanged for everyone.
Malformed email → 400 'Please enter a valid email address.'
GET /api/waitlist/info?ref=surya-378738 → {valid:true, referrer_name:'Surya', referrer_avatar:...}. Unknown ref → {valid:false}. Missing ref param → {valid:false}.
GET /api/referrals exposes stats.waitlist_signups (count of via=waitlist attributions) and the referees list includes status=waitlist rows with aed_earned=25 and tier='Waitlist'.

Backend logs after a successful POST /api/waitlist/join show 'support email sent ... to=surya@onex.exchange' (admin notification). Visitor welcome emails are dispatched too but Resend can rate-limit them under burst-test conditions (5 req/sec cap).

Frontend /progress at 1920x1080: '7 / 12 Steps Completed' summary, 5 lock icons (= 5 upcoming steps), 11 +AED reward pills (allocation_ready has 0 reward so no pill), Auto badge visible on pending auto step ('Browse Dubai Properties · +AED 10 · AUTO · PENDING'), Mark Complete button correctly hidden (only pending step is auto). All 12 milestone test-ids present in DOM.

Frontend /invite has data-testid='referral-tab-waitlist' rendering 'Waitlist · 7'. Clicking it filters the table to exactly 7 status='waitlist' rows; each shows 'Joined waitlist' pink chip + 'Waitlist' tier + '+AED 25'.

Frontend /invite has data-testid='framer-embed-section' ('Capture emails on your Framer site') with framer-copy-link and framer-copy-snippet test-ids. Both buttons copy to clipboard and show success toasts ('Framer embed code copied to clipboard').

Snippet contents verified character-for-character via navigator.clipboard.readText(): contains <form id="onex-wl-form", fetch(API+"/waitlist/join", /waitlist/info?ref=, and the resolved REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL host 'exclusive-members-1.preview.emergentagent.com'. Total length 2867 chars.
No regression: /api/dashboard, /api/referrals, /api/webinars, /api/leaderboard all return 200 with valid dict shapes.
action_items: (Optional, non-blocking) Add retry-with-backoff in email_service when Resend returns RateLimitError so the visitor welcome email survives bursty traffic. Admin notification path is unaffected.

(Optional, doc clarity) The cascade invariant 'all steps after pending are upcoming' is implemented as 'all NON-COMPLETED steps after pending are upcoming' because save_property / invite_friend / friend_kyc / etc. can data-complete out of order. The current behavior is the correct UX (don't punish users for jumping ahead) — just update the spec wording so future testers don't read it as a bug.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2040 lines (was ~1780 last iteration). Split waitlist + referrals + progress into separate routers when convenient — single-file growth is the only smell left.

auto_complete_data_milestones runs eight async count_documents queries on every GET /api/progress and GET /api/dashboard. For a hot dashboard endpoint, consider an in-memory TTL cache (e.g., 30s) keyed by user_id, or fold the checks into a single aggregation pipeline.

MILESTONE_REWARDS is duplicated knowledge with the frontend STEP_REWARDS constant in MyProgress.js — drift risk. Expose a /api/progress/rewards static endpoint or hard-include the reward inside each milestone object so the frontend stops shipping its own copy.
/api/waitlist/join silently ignores malformed payload.source values; if a partner sends garbage we'll store it verbatim. Add an allow-list (framer, landing, manual) or whitelist via Pydantic enum.

buildFramerSnippet() inlines the production API base into the copied HTML — fine, but it means a Framer block copied today will permanently point to today's host. If REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL ever rotates, embedded forms in customer Framer sites silently break. Document this caveat in the embed UI.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — every assertion in the spec verified live in the browser via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL

test_credentials: Bearer/cookie session_token=test_2a912b090cba495aa681e8794bacb6fb (Surya, referral_code=surya-378738). Pre-existing waitlist row test-waitlist-1@example.com NOT re-tested.
seed_data_creation: Created ~7 transient waitlist_signups rows with random UUIDs (fresh-test-*, dup-test-*, badref-*, wl-stat-*). These are intentional and visible in the Waitlist tab as Joined-waitlist rows attributed to Surya. Also toggled saved_properties on/off for a TEST_prop_* id (cleaned up by the test itself).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite for tasks 1 + 2 lives at /app/backend/tests/test_waitlist_and_progress.py and is independent of the larger backend_test.py. Run with: pytest backend/tests/test_waitlist_and_progress.py -v. The seeded user (user_qa_1781303183733) already has 7/12 milestones completed and accumulated test-side waitlist signups — when re-running, the test that 'manually completes attend_webinar' will continue to skip until the user is reset or a different manual-pending step is targeted. Stripe still flagged DUMMY by main agent — out of scope.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA. Two transient test-assertion errors during scaffolding (wrong icon literal, wrong key 'completed' vs 'completed_count') were corrected in test_waitlist_and_progress.py — the implementation was correct.
